member.name: Pat McFadden

addresses: Crescent House, Broad Street, Bilston, WV14 0BZ

member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Pat McFadden is the Labour MP for Wolverhampton South East , and has been an MP continually since 5 May 2005. He currently holds the Government post of Secretary of State for Work and Pensions.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office: ICT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, with reference to the policy paper entitled Transforming for a digital future: 2022 to 2025 roadmap for digital and data, updated on 29 February 2024, when his Department first assessed each of the red-rated leg
writtenQuestions.answerText: Cabinet Office Digital first carried out an audit between September 2022 and Feb 2023, as part of the work to upgrade our IT estate. It has created a framework for managing legacy systems in the department and we are proactively working with the Central D...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Work and Pensions: ICT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, with reference to the policy paper entitled Transforming for a digital future: 2022 to 2025 roadmap for digital and data, updated on 29 February 2024, when his Department first assessed each of the red-
writtenQuestions.answerText: DWP has mature, industry standard mechanisms for assessing the risk of its core IT systems which has been implemented and running for decades. The first time DWP reported figures to the cross-government initiative led by CDDO was November 2022.

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office: ICT

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Central Digital and Data Office (CDDO), in the Cabinet Office, has established a programme to support departments managing legacy IT. CDDO has agreed a framework to identify ‘red-rated’ systems, indicating high levels of risk surrounding certain asset...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Work and Pensions: ICT

writtenQuestions.answerText: DWP has funded and resourced a dedicated Legacy Technical Debt Working Group within DWP. This group managed the population and ongoing accuracy of Legacy IT System Risk Score Cards. The information recorded includes plans for mitigating key risks which ar...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Education: Domestic Visits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, to which domestic destinations Ministers in her Department have attended overnight visits in each of the last three financial years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the right hon. Member for Wolverhampton South East to the answer of 12 March 2024 to Question 17517.

writtenQuestions.heading: Sickness Benefits: Wolverhampton
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many people in Wolverhampton South East constituency are on long-term sickness benefits; and how many were on such benefits in each of the past five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: People on ‘long-term sickness benefits’ have been assumed as those in the main phase of Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) and the Universal Credit (UC) Limited Capability to Work (LCW) and Limited capability for work and work-related activity (LCWRA)...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Education: Fraud and Maladministration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what estimate she has made of the amount of money lost to fraud and error by her Department in each of the last three financial years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is proud of its record in proactively seeking to find and prevent more fraud in the system. The government established the dedicated Public Sector Fraud Authority (PSFA). In its first year it delivered £311 million in audited counter fraud ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office: WhatsApp
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what guidance his Department issues on the use of WhatsApp.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In common with all other departments in central government, and arms lengths bodies, Cabinet Office applies the published guidance: Using Non-Corporate Communication Channels (e.g. WhatsApp, private email, SMS, etc.) for Government Business, found here: h...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Education: Advertising
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how much funding her Department and has (a) budgeted for and (b) spent on advertising in each of the last three financial years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department provides details of the funding spent on advertising and publicity as part of its annual reports and accounts, which are published in the usual way on GOV.UK:https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/department-for-education-consolidated-a...
